fields.narrative_intro: *A manic person and a sulking person discuss the first decoded Herculaneum scroll at an AI-powered drive-through that keeps misunderstanding every order.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
MANIC PERSON: "AI read a scroll sealed since Vesuvius, 79 AD!"
SULKING PERSON: "Two thousand years. What's it say?"
fields.panel2:
[build]
MANIC PERSON: "A Stoic treatise: 'we depart from ourselves...'"
SPEAKER BOX: "DID YOU SAY TWO MCFLURRIES FOR ARISTOCREON?"
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
MANIC PERSON: "No! Chrysippus's nephew, Aristocreon, the philosopher—"
SPEAKER BOX: "ADDING NEPHEW. ANYTHING ELSE, CHRYSIPPUS?"
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: Both blobs stare flatly at the speaker box. A carbonized receipt slowly extrudes from the slot, curling like a tiny scroll.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
SULKING PERSON: "It got 22 columns off a charred scroll."
MANIC PERSON: "And zero columns off my order."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: The receipt unfurls, ink barely visible: 'we will not grasp it, if in some way we depart from ourselves... — no pickles.']

narrative_summary: An AI that flawlessly decoded a 2,000-year-old Stoic scroll cannot understand a drive-through order, mangling the real names and quotes onto a charred receipt.
rationale: Candidate 2 weaves the real names (Aristocreon, Chrysippus) and the actual quote into the drive-through mishearing engine itself, so every panel escalates the joke and the final receipt button pays off both the scroll and the order. Candidate 4 ties for total but reuses the quote as a single gag rather than building a structural through-line, making 2 the cleaner, denser execution.
story_title: An entire Herculaneum scroll has been read for the first time
place: at an AI-powered drive-through that keeps misunderstanding every order
hat_pair: foam hot air balloon basket hat with a tiny balloon floating on top
